title: artemisia ludoviciana subsp. gnaphalodes
intro: Artemisia ludoviciana subsp. gnaphalodes, also known as Western mugwort, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its silvery-gray foliage and tall, branching stems. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve menstrual cramps, and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Western mugwort include its potential to alleviate symptoms of menopause, improve sleep quality, and support the immune system. Western mugwort can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, making it a versatile herbal remedy for various health concerns.
